text.title: Automated Persona Pipeline Agent Workflow
text.summary: Haiku/Opus/Sonnet roles, silhouette gate rules, structured JSON persona output, and gotchas for fully automated persona pipelines.

rule.statement: Haiku: ingest behavioral event exports via API, normalize to feature matrix, compute cluster centroids. Do not delegate normalization to Sonnet or Opus.
rule.rationale: Normalization is mechanical and deterministic. Using higher-capability models for mechanical data processing wastes cost without quality benefit.

rule.statement: Opus: validate cluster separability (silhouette score), generate cluster report, flag fragile clusters (under 50 users) and redundant clusters (over 70% feature overlap).
rule.rationale: Cluster boundary identification requires reasoning about feature distributions; Opus provides the analytical depth needed for reliable cluster quality assessment.

rule.statement: Sonnet: generate persona narratives, JTBD maps, and pain-point summaries only after human approval of Opus cluster report.
rule.rationale: Narrative generation is expensive to redo. Running Sonnet before human approval of clusters wastes tokens and produces personas built on potentially invalid segments.

rule.statement: When silhouette scores are below 0.25, the pipeline must abort and surface the issue rather than proceeding to narrative generation.
rule.rationale: Pipeline auto-retry on weak clusters compounds the error. Weak clusters produce personas with confidence language that stakeholders accept uncritically.

rule.statement: Run clustering with at least 3 different random seeds; if membership changes significantly across seeds, reject the k and flag for human data review.
rule.rationale: Unstable clusters signal that the feature set does not support the chosen segmentation. The solution is better feature engineering, not narrative generation from bad segments.

rule.statement: Agents generating JTBD statements from behavioral data without qualitative input produce feature descriptions dressed as motivations; require an "inferred" tag or reject.
rule.rationale: JTBD requires motivation (why), which is absent from analytics exports. Behavioral data alone produces statements like "wants to export data" when the real motivation is unstated.

rule.statement: Fully automated pipeline without a human gate between clustering and narrative generation is high-risk: a bad cluster produces a confidently-written persona that gets adopted without scrutiny.
rule.rationale: The entire value of data-driven personas collapses if the segments are invalid. The human gate is the last control before personas enter production systems.

text.reference: Silhouette scoring with abort on weak clusters
Prompt for structured persona JSON with data_confidence and assumptions fields
